conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry, The University of Memphis, Memphis, Tennessee 38154, United States
conditions.evidence_text: Analytical LCCC analysis used a column (Nucleosil C18, 250 × 4.6 mm, 100 Å, 5 μm) smaller than the preparative fractionation. The eluent was CH2Cl2/CH3CN (58/42, v/v) at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min. The column temperature was varied to achieve CAP condition for the polymers of interest. TCAP and tE,CAP of Ls-PS... are measured as 14.8 °C/7.4 min
conditions.notes: Ls-PS refers to linear PS with saturated DPE end-groups. Used to establish CAP for linear polymers.

conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry, The University of Memphis, Memphis, Tennessee 38154, United States
conditions.evidence_text: Analytical LCCC analysis used a column (Nucleosil C18, 250 × 4.6 mm, 100 Å, 5 μm)... The eluent was CH2Cl2/CH3CN (58/42, v/v) at a flow rate of 0.5 mL/min. The column temperature was varied to achieve CAP condition... TCAP and tE,CAP of... Lu-PS... are measured as 14.95 °C/7.6 min
conditions.notes: Lu-PS refers to linear PS with unsaturated DPE end-groups. Used to establish CAP for linear polymers with reactive end-groups.

conditions.evidence_text: For fractionation of ring PS, two C18 bonded silica columns (Nucleosil C18, 250 × 9.8 mm, 100 Å pore, 5 μm and Nucleosil C18, 250 × 9.8 mm, 300 Å pore, 5 μm) and a mixed RT eluent of CH2Cl2/CH3CN (58/42, v/v, Samchun, HPLC grade) at a FD flow rate of 2 mL/min were used. The temperature of the columns was set at 19 °C at which different molar mass linear PS precursors coelute.
